title: Salted caramel brownies
subtitle: Fudgy dark chocolate brownies with a salted caramel swirl
excerpt: Fudgy dark chocolate brownies with a ribbon of salted caramel swirled through the batter before baking. The caramel is made first, cooled until thick, then spooned over the brownie batter and swirled in with a skewer. Sea salt on top. Cut into 16.
sourceNotes: Chocolate brownies with caramel additions are documented in American domestic baking from the early twentieth century, where caramel sauce as a brownie filling or topping appears in household baking manuals available through the Internet Archive. The salted caramel variant builds on the French tradition of salted butter caramel (caramel au beurre salé) documented in Norman and Breton regional cooking, and in European domestic baking literature from the late twentieth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze cut brownies individually wrapped. Defrost at room temperature for 1–2 hours.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Improve with a day of rest at room temperature. Keep for 3 days at room temperature or 5 days refrigerated.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf. The caramel swirl makes the top set unevenly — that is expected. The edges should be set and dry at 28 minutes; the centre should still be soft

glossaryTerms.definition: The technique of spooning cooled caramel over an unbaked batter and dragging a skewer through it in S-shapes to create a marbled pattern. The caramel must be thick enough to hold its shape when spooned — if still warm and liquid it will sink into the batter and disappear rather than swirling. Cool the caramel at room temperature until it holds a ribbon when poured, then work quickly before it stiffens further.

content.text: The caramel must be ready before you make the brownie batter: it needs time to cool and thicken to a spoonable consistency. Make the caramel first, set it aside, then mix the brownie batter while the caramel reaches the right thickness. Rushing it and swirling while still hot and liquid produces a caramel that sinks and disappears rather than swirls.

Melt the {{caster-sugar}} for the caramel in a small saucepan over medium heat without stirring until it turns a deep amber colour. Remove from the heat and carefully pour in the warmed {{double-cream}} for the caramel — it will spit. Stir until smooth. Add the {{unsalted-butter}} for the caramel and {{sea-salt-fine}} for the caramel and stir until combined. Pour into a bowl and leave to cool at room temperature until thick and spoonable, about 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 170°C fan. Butter and line a 20 cm square cake tin. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} and {{unsalted-butter}} for the brownies together in a small saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Cool for 5 minutes. Whisk in the {{caster-sugar}} and {{light-brown-sugar}} for the brownies. Whisk in the {{eggs}} one at a time, then the {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the sifted {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} for the brownies until just combined. Pour into the prepared tin.
Spoon the cooled caramel over the brownie batter in large spoonfuls. Use a skewer or the tip of a knife to create the

: drag the skewer through the batter in S-shapes, pulling the caramel through without fully mixing it in. Bake for 26–28 minutes until the edges are set and the centre is still soft. Cool completely in the tin before cutting — at least 30 minutes.
